items.title: Enhance Image Handling on Publish in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces automatic re-hosting of external images for CSDN, Medium, and Bilibili during the publishing process. Each platform's images are now uploaded to their respective CDNs, ensuring articles are not rejected due to hotlinking issues. The implementation includes graceful degradation for image upload failures, enhancing the overall user experience.

items.title: Enhance Bilibili Draft Management & Medium Auto-Retry in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces new commands for managing Bilibili drafts, allowing users to list and delete drafts to avoid hitting the 999 draft limit. Additionally, it implements an auto-retry mechanism for Medium's transient 403 errors, ensuring smoother operations when fetching drafts. These enhancements improve user experience and reliability in the Skills area.

items.title: Fix token mapping for LinkedIn and Reddit in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves the issue where the LinkedIn and Reddit tokens were not correctly mapped in the worker's environment variable configuration. The missing mappings led to empty access tokens and subsequent API errors. With these fixes, both services are now properly authenticated and operational, ensuring seamless integration.

items.title: Fix OAuth Scope Fallback for Connectors in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves a critical issue where Microsoft Teams was not receiving the correct Chat scopes during reconnections. The fix ensures that when no explicit scopes are provided, the system defaults to the connector's declared permissions instead of the adapter's base scopes. Additionally, two new tests have been added to verify the fallback behavior and scope narrowing functionality.

items.title: Optimize Memory Usage for Mobile Safari in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update implements critical memory optimizations to prevent out-of-memory crashes in mobile Safari when rendering large transcripts. Key changes include limiting the number of rendered events and truncating oversized content, ensuring smoother performance during extensive sessions. These adjustments aim to enhance user experience by maintaining stability across devices.

items.title: Fix HTTP Port Binding for Serp Worker in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update explicitly sets the `HTTP_PORT` environment variable to `8000` in both production and test deployments. This change ensures that newly built Serp images bind to the correct port, preventing crash loops and allowing for successful health checks. As a result, the `/serp/google` endpoint now returns the expected cost data, enabling the full functionality of the service.

items.title: Streamlined Curl Auth for Skills in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update replaces Bash arrays with a more portable syntax for curl authentication headers across 11 skills, ensuring compatibility with sh/dash environments. The changes enhance reliability in executing API calls without syntax errors, improving overall functionality. No frontmatter or scope changes were made.

items.title: Launch LinkedIn OAuth Integration in Backend 🚀
items.summary: The LinkedIn OAuth app has been successfully registered, and the necessary secrets have been created in the cluster. Key changes include updates to the deployment configuration and the creation of a script to manage the LinkedIn OAuth secrets. This integration allows users to authenticate via LinkedIn, enhancing the authentication options available in the application.

items.title: Refactor deploy directory names for clarity in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: The box-deploy directories have been renamed to reflect the actual service names, enhancing clarity and reducing confusion. The changes include renaming `gpu-box` directories to `platform-service-maestro-worker` and `platform-service-digitalhuman-worker`. All relevant Docker compose files and documentation have been updated accordingly, ensuring that the services validate and run smoothly under their new names.

items.title: Align Figma OAuth Scopes for Project Metadata in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update corrects the OAuth scope for the Figma app by replacing the invalid `projects:read` with the correct `project_metadata:read`. The changes ensure that the connector permissions and adapter default scopes are aligned with the officially registered scopes, improving the authorization process. All Figma adapter tests have successfully passed.

items.title: Enhance mobile Safari stability and clean URL handling in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a critical issue causing the coding-bridge page to crash on mobile Safari by throttling the streaming markdown rendering. Additionally, it removes the session ID from the URL, ensuring a cleaner and more user-friendly experience. These changes improve performance and maintain session integrity during refreshes.

items.title: Streamlined Box Uploads to COS in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update refactors the upload process for both the maestro and digital-human services, allowing them to upload finished MP4 files directly to COS without size limitations. The previous files-API has been removed, ensuring a more efficient and standardized approach across services. Key environment variables have been updated to support this new configuration, enhancing overall performance and reliability.

items.title: Launch Figma OAuth App in Backend 🚀
items.summary: The Figma OAuth app has been successfully registered, and the `oauth-figma` secret is now created in-cluster. This update will restart the auth-backend to load the new FIGMA_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID and SECRET, making the Figma connector visible in the catalog. Other connectors like TikTok and Google remain hidden for now.

items.title: Refresh icons for Microsoft and Google connectors in Backend 🎨
items.summary: This update introduces a complete refresh of icons for nine connectors, aligning with the recent redesigns from Microsoft and Google. The Microsoft Fluent icons have been updated for Excel and Teams, while Google Workspace connectors now feature the new gradient icons. These changes enhance visual consistency and modernize the user interface.

items.title: Fix namespaced skill slug handling in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses a critical bug affecting namespaced skill slugs by ensuring that the asset bundle and sandbox SKILL_DIR paths align correctly. A new function, `sandboxSkillSlug`, strips slashes from skill slugs to prevent path mismatches, which previously led to script resolution failures. Comprehensive tests confirm that the fix is effective without impacting single-segment slugs.

items.title: Set up Figma and TikTok OAuth secrets in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update pre-wires the production deployment for Figma and TikTok OAuth connectors, allowing for easier integration once the OAuth apps are registered. The changes include modifications to the create-secrets.sh script and the deployment.yaml file, ensuring that the necessary Kubernetes secrets can be created seamlessly. Pods will remain healthy until the secrets are available, keeping the connectors hidden in the meantime.

items.title: Enhance Per-Call Cost Reporting in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update extends the per-call cost feature to all remaining billed generation providers across 13 task workers, ensuring a consistent API response for partner settlement ledgers. Key changes include the introduction of a read-only fetchPrice method and embedding cost information into the success results of each worker. The implementation follows a proven pattern, enhancing billing accuracy without impacting response times.
